title: FIFA World Cup 2026 — List of injured players who will miss the tournament
original_text: FIFA World Cup 2026 — List of injured players who will miss the tournament left Hugo Ekitike, Fermin Lopez and Rodrygo are among the major absentees at FIFA World Cup 2026. AFP https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/320vdh/article71077680.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/Injured%20players%20FIFA%20World%20Cup.jpg AFP While a total of 1248 players will be travelling to North America, many have been left out due to unfortunate injuries that they picked up during the club season. Chennai The FIFA World Cup 2026 is close on the heels as 48 teams prepare to fight it out in the USA, Canada and Mexico for the biggest prize in football. The tournament will begin on July 11 with co-host Mexico taking on South Africa at the Mexico City Stadium, while the final will be played on July 19 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. While a total of 1248 players will be travelling to North America, many have been left out due to unfortunate injuries that they picked up during the club season. Take a look at the major names that will be missing from FIFA World Cup 2026 due to injury: Xavi Simons (Netherlands) Tottenham Hotspur midfielder Xavi Simons’ season came to an end in April during his side’s Premier League 2025-26 match against Wolverhampton Wanderers. The 23-year-old ruptured the Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) in his right knee during the second half of the game and underwent surgery, which made him unavailable for Netherlands at the World Cup. Rodrygo (Brazil) Brazilian Rodrygo was all set to reunite with Carlo Ancelotti for the World Cup, but the Real Madrid winger was diagnosed with a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament and a tear of the lateral meniscus in his right leg, an injury which he sustained during a league match against Getafe in March. Hugo Ekitike (France) Following Olivier Giroud’s international retirement in 2024, France has been in search of a conventional striker, with Hugo Ekitike emerging as the successor. However, the 23-year-old ruptured his Achilles tendon while playing for Liverpool against Paris Saint-Germain in the UEFA Champions League, forcing him to miss the summer tournament. Fermin Lopez (Spain) Spanish midfielder Fermin Lopez was hoping to make the squad after an impressive season with Barcelona, scoring 13 goals and grabbing 17 assists ‌across all competitions as the club won La Liga ​and the Spanish Super Cup. But he suffered a fracture to the fifth metatarsal bone in his right foot during a La Liga game against Real Betis last month, ruling him out of the World Cup. Serge Gnabry (Germany) With 26 goals in 59 appearances, Serge Gnabry was set to enter the World Cup as Germany’s active top scorer. However, the 30-year-old suffered an adductor muscle tear in his right leg in April, which ruled him out of the rest of Bayern Munich’s season as well as the quadrennial tournament. Matthijs de Ligt (Netherlands) Manchester United defender Matthijs de Ligt made a positive start to this season, playing every minute of the club’s first 13 Premier League games until a back injury halted his progress. Last month, he underwent surgery and is not expected to return until the early stages of next season. Estevao (Brazil) Brazilian wonderkid Estevao was primed to have a spot in Carlo Ancelotti’s squad for the World Cup, scoring five goals in his last six games for Brazil. The 19-year-old, however, suffered a hamstring injury while playing for Chelsea against Manchester United in April and was ruled out for the rest of the season. Kaoru Mitoma (Japan) Japan’s bid to make it past the round of 16 for the first time was dealt a huge blow after winger Kaoru Mitoma was ruled out of the tournament after picking up a hamstring injury in Brighton’s match against Wolves. Lennart Karl (Germany) After a breakout season where he became Bayern Munich’s youngest goalscorer in the Champions League, Lennart Karl was rightfully called up by Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann. However, tragedy struck when the 18-year-old sustained a muscle tear in training ahead of Germany’s friendly against USA. RB Leipzig’s Assan Ouedraogo has been named his replacement. Wesley (Brazil) Brazil will take part in the World Cup without a specialist right-back after AS Roma’s Wesley suffered a thigh injury during a warm-up game against Egypt. He has been replaced by Atalanta midfielder Ederson. Billy Gilmour (Scotland) Billy Gilmour was a prominent figure in Scotland’s World Cup qualifying campaign but will not play a part in the Finals as he sustained a knee injury during Scotland’s final World Cup warm-up ‌match against Curacao. Jurrien Timber (Netherlands) Jurrien Timber has had an impressive season with Arsenal, helping the club lift its first league title in 22 years. He was a constant in its starting lineup for the majority of the season before injuries forced him to be sidelined towards the business end. With the 24-year-old failing to recover from an issue in his groin area, he was let go from the Netherlands camp three days before the start of the World Cup and has been replaced by Sunderland’s Lutsharel Geertruida.
